title: Staining of CD31 and CD13 in PDGFR-B/Td-tomato brain sections
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"82n0m","text":"This protocol is suitable for the staining of CD31 and CD13 in PDGFR-B/Td-Tomato fixed mouse brain sections.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"bq7uq","text":"Read the entire protocol before starting the procedure.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7gnau","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cv89p","text":"Note that this protocol uses free-floating sections to enhance CD31 staining. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"44ij8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"71pii","text":"This protocol uses sequential staining to avoid cross-reactivity between CD31 and CD13.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7qdn7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cvlqj","text":"Do not submit the tissue to antigen retrieval or other acidic solutions to preserve theTd-tomato signal.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3iajp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1nf0c","text":"Works with the listed antibodies and dilutions. For other references, preliminary tests are highly recommended. steps: Extract the sections from the anti-freeze media and rinse them in PBS using a 24-well plate.
Aspirate the PBS and incubate the sections in the Blocking solution for 01:00:00 at Room temperature .

When blocking is finished, aspirate the buffer (no washing is required) and incubate CD31 (BD Bioscience, 550274, 1:200) in antibody buffer for Overnight at 4 °C . Since the sections are already permeabilized, please note that antibody buffers do not contain Triton X-100 Please note that the proposed antibody incubation includes only CD31. CD13 will be incubated after.
When primary antibody incubation is finished, aspirate the media and wash the sections 00:05:00, 5x with 0.05 % (v/v) TWEEN 20 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P7949 in PBS.
Incubate Donkey α Goat 488 (Invitrogen, A-11055, 1:500) secondary antibody in 0.1 % (v/v) TWEEN 20 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P7949 for 01:00:00 at Room temperature

When blocking is finished, aspirate the buffer (no washing is required) and incubate CD13 (R&D, AF2335, 1:300) in antibody buffer for 03:00:00 at Room temperature . Please note that this antibody incubation is performed differently from the first one (3 hours at room temperature).
Clean the remaining buffer on the slides using absorbent tissue and mount the sections with a drop of Fluoromount-G Electron Microscopy Sciences Catalog #17984-25 (RRID: AB_2572296) .
When secondary antibody incubation is finished, aspirate the media and wash the sections 00:05:00, 5x with 0.05 % (v/v) TWEEN 20 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P7949 in PBS.
Perform a second blocking step using 5% Donkey serum in 0.1 % (v/v) TWEEN 20 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P7949 in PBS.

When secondary antibody incubation is finished, wash the sections 00:05:00, 3x with 0.05 % (v/v) TWEEN 20 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P7949 in PBS. Follow this with 00:05:00, 2x washes with PBS to remove all detergent traces.
When primary antibody incubation is finished, aspirate the media and wash the sections 00:05:00, 5x with 0.05 % (v/v) TWEEN 20 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P7949 in PBS.

Incubate Donkey α Rat 647 (Jackson, 712-605-153, 1:300) secondary antibody in 0.1 % (v/v) TWEEN 20 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P7949 for 01:00:00 at Room temperature . Add DAPI (Invitrogen, D3571, 1:5000)
creator: Daniel Manrique-Castano
